# Tarot Card Iconography: Major & Minor Arcana Reference

> Source: labyrinthos.co, tarot.com, wikipedia.org (Major Arcana, Minor Arcana), astrology.com
> Fetched: 2026-03-27

---

## 1. Overview

The standard tarot deck contains **78 cards** divided into two main sections:
- **Major Arcana**: 22 cards (numbered 0-21) representing major life themes and spiritual archetypes
- **Minor Arcana**: 56 cards divided into 4 suits of 14 cards each, representing everyday situations

The Major Arcana tells the story of the **Fool's Journey** - humanity's spiritual evolution from innocent wonder (The Fool, 0) to integration and fulfillment (The World, 21).

---

## 2. Major Arcana (22 Cards)

### The Fool's Journey Narrative

The 22 Major Arcana cards follow a sequential narrative arc:
- **Cards 0-7**: Formation of identity and worldly experience
- **Cards 8-14**: Internal growth and self-mastery
- **Cards 15-21**: Spiritual transformation and transcendence

### Complete Major Arcana Reference

| # | Card | Upright Meaning | Reversed Meaning | Symbolism & Iconography |
|---|------|----------------|------------------|------------------------|
| 0 | **The Fool** | Innocence, new beginnings, free spirit, leap of faith | Recklessness, being taken advantage of, naivety | Standing at cliff edge; open-hearted freedom; trusting the unknown; a small dog companion; white rose of purity; bundle of possessions |
| 1 | **The Magician** | Willpower, desire, creation, manifestation | Trickery, illusions, out of touch, manipulation | One hand raised to sky, one pointing to earth; infinity symbol; table with four suit symbols (wand, cup, sword, pentacle); red and white robes |
| 2 | **The High Priestess** | Intuition, unconscious, inner voice, hidden knowledge | Lack of center, lost inner voice, repressed feelings | Seated between two pillars (B and J); crescent moon at feet; scroll of Torah/wisdom; veil with pomegranates; blue robes |
| 3 | **The Empress** | Motherhood, fertility, nature, abundance, creativity | Dependence, smothering, emptiness, creative block | Lush garden setting; crown of twelve stars; Venus symbol on shield; flowing river; wheat/grain at feet; comfortable throne |
| 4 | **The Emperor** | Authority, structure, control, fatherhood, discipline | Tyranny, rigidity, coldness, excessive control | Stone throne with ram heads (Aries); red robes; long white beard; orb and ankh/scepter; mountain backdrop; armor beneath robes |
| 5 | **The Hierophant** | Tradition, conformity, morality, ethics, spiritual guidance | Rebellion, subversiveness, new approaches, challenging tradition | Religious figure between two pillars; raised hand in blessing; crossed keys at feet; two acolytes; triple crown; red and white vestments |
| 6 | **The Lovers** | Partnerships, duality, union, choices, alignment | Loss of balance, one-sidedness, disharmony, misalignment | Man and woman beneath angel; Tree of Knowledge (woman) and Tree of Life (man); mountain between them; angel's blessing from above |
| 7 | **The Chariot** | Direction, control, willpower, determination, victory | Lack of control, lack of direction, aggression, defeat | Armored figure in chariot; two sphinxes (black and white); starry canopy; city behind; crescent moons on shoulders; square on chest |
| 8 | **Strength** | Inner strength, bravery, compassion, focus, patience | Self-doubt, weakness, insecurity, raw emotion | Woman gently holding lion's jaw; infinity symbol above head; white robes; flower garland; calm expression; gentle dominance over beast |
| 9 | **The Hermit** | Contemplation, search for truth, inner guidance, solitude | Loneliness, isolation, lost your way, withdrawal | Cloaked figure on mountain peak; lantern with six-pointed star; staff of wisdom; grey robes; snow-capped mountains; looking down from height |
| 10 | **Wheel of Fortune** | Change, cycles, inevitable fate, destiny, turning point | No control, clinging to control, bad luck, resistance to change | Great wheel with Hebrew letters (YHVH); four figures in corners (lion, eagle, bull, angel = four evangelists); sphinx atop wheel; Anubis descending; snake ascending |
| 11 | **Justice** | Cause and effect, clarity, truth, fairness, accountability | Dishonesty, unaccountability, unfairness, avoidance | Seated figure with scales and sword; crown; red robes; purple veil behind; two grey pillars; one foot visible beneath robes |
| 12 | **The Hanged Man** | Sacrifice, release, martyrdom, new perspective, pause | Stalling, needless sacrifice, fear of sacrifice, resistance | Figure suspended upside-down from T-shaped cross; serene expression; halo of light; one leg crossed behind other; hands behind back |
| 13 | **Death** | End of cycle, beginnings, change, metamorphosis, transformation | Fear of change, holding on, stagnation, decay, resistance | Skeleton in black armor on white horse; fallen king; praying figures; bishop; child offering flowers; rising sun between two towers; river flowing |
| 14 | **Temperance** | Middle path, patience, finding meaning, balance, moderation | Extremes, excess, lack of balance, impatience | Angel with one foot on land, one in water; pouring water between two cups; triangle on chest; irises growing; path to mountains; sun/crown on horizon |
| 15 | **The Devil** | Addiction, materialism, playfulness, bondage, shadow self | Freedom, release, restoring control, breaking chains | Baphomet figure on pedestal; inverted pentagram; two chained figures (man and woman); chains are loose (could be removed); bat wings; raised torch |
| 16 | **The Tower** | Sudden upheaval, broken pride, disaster, revelation | Disaster avoided, delayed disaster, fear of suffering | Tall tower struck by lightning; crown blown off; two figures falling; flames from windows; 22 drops of flame (Hebrew letters); dark sky; rocky foundation |
| 17 | **The Star** | Hope, faith, rejuvenation, inspiration, serenity | Faithlessness, discouragement, insecurity, disconnection | Naked woman kneeling by pool; one foot on water, one on land; pouring water from two jugs; eight-pointed star above; seven smaller stars; ibis bird in tree |
| 18 | **The Moon** | Unconscious, illusions, intuition, fear, anxiety | Confusion, fear, misinterpretation, clarity emerging | Full moon with face; dog and wolf howling; crayfish emerging from water; path between two towers; drops of dew falling; rolling hills |
| 19 | **The Sun** | Joy, success, celebration, positivity, vitality | Negativity, depression, sadness, temporary setback | Large bright sun; sunflowers; naked child on white horse; red banner; stone wall; pure joy and innocence |
| 20 | **Judgement** | Reflection, reckoning, awakening, rebirth, calling | Lack of self-awareness, doubt, self-loathing, fear of death | Angel blowing trumpet; figures rising from coffins; mountains in background; flag with cross; tidal wave of sound; family groups rising |
| 21 | **The World** | Fulfillment, harmony, completion, integration, achievement | Incompletion, no closure, shortcuts, stagnation | Dancing figure in laurel wreath; four creatures in corners (same as Wheel of Fortune); two wands; flowing purple scarf; oval wreath |

---

## 3. Minor Arcana Overview

### The Four Suits

| Suit | Element | Domain | Season | Direction | Class |
|------|---------|--------|--------|-----------|-------|
| **Wands** | Fire | Passion, inspiration, willpower, ambition | Spring | South | Creative/Spiritual |
| **Cups** | Water | Emotions, intuition, relationships, creativity | Summer | West | Emotional/Relational |
| **Swords** | Air | Intellect, logic, truth, conflict, communication | Autumn | East | Mental/Intellectual |
| **Pentacles** | Earth | Material, finances, health, work, nature | Winter | North | Physical/Material |

### Court Card Hierarchy

| Rank | Role | Maturity | Represents |
|------|------|----------|------------|
| **Page** | Student/Messenger | Youth | Curiosity, learning, potential |
| **Knight** | Warrior/Quester | Young adult | Action, pursuit, extremes |
| **Queen** | Nurturer/Inward mastery | Mature | Embodiment, internal mastery |
| **King** | Leader/Outward mastery | Elder | Command, external mastery |

---

## 4. Suit of Wands (Fire)

**Theme**: Passion, inspiration, willpower, ambition, creativity, energy

| Card | Upright | Reversed |
|------|---------|----------|
| **Ace of Wands** | Creation, willpower, inspiration, desire | Lack of energy, lack of passion, boredom |
| **Two of Wands** | Planning, making decisions, leaving home | Fear of change, playing safe, bad planning |
| **Three of Wands** | Looking ahead, expansion, rapid growth | Obstacles, delays, frustration |
| **Four of Wands** | Community, home, celebration | Lack of support, transience, home conflicts |
| **Five of Wands** | Competition, rivalry, conflict | Avoiding conflict, respecting differences |
| **Six of Wands** | Victory, success, public reward | Excess pride, lack of recognition, punishment |
| **Seven of Wands** | Perseverance, defensive, maintaining control | Giving up, destroyed confidence, overwhelmed |
| **Eight of Wands** | Rapid action, movement, quick decisions | Panic, waiting, slowdown |
| **Nine of Wands** | Resilience, grit, last stand | Exhaustion, fatigue, questioning motivations |
| **Ten of Wands** | Accomplishment, responsibility, burden | Inability to delegate, overstressed, burnt out |
| **Page of Wands** | Exploration, excitement, freedom | Lack of direction, procrastination, creating conflict |
| **Knight of Wands** | Action, adventure, fearlessness | Anger, impulsiveness, recklessness |
| **Queen of Wands** | Courage, determination, joy | Selfishness, jealousy, insecurities |
| **King of Wands** | Big picture, leader, overcoming challenges | Impulsive, overbearing, unachievable expectations |

---

## 5. Suit of Cups (Water)

**Theme**: Emotions, the unconscious, creativity, intuition, relationships, feelings

| Card | Upright | Reversed |
|------|---------|----------|
| **Ace of Cups** | New feelings, spirituality, intuition | Emotional loss, blocked creativity, emptiness |
| **Two of Cups** | Unity, partnership, connection | Imbalance, broken communication, tension |
| **Three of Cups** | Friendship, community, happiness | Overindulgence, gossip, isolation |
| **Four of Cups** | Apathy, contemplation, disconnectedness | Sudden awareness, choosing happiness, acceptance |
| **Five of Cups** | Loss, grief, self-pity | Acceptance, moving on, finding peace |
| **Six of Cups** | Familiarity, happy memories, healing | Moving forward, leaving home, independence |
| **Seven of Cups** | Searching for purpose, choices, daydreaming | Lack of purpose, diversion, confusion |
| **Eight of Cups** | Walking away, disillusionment, leaving behind | Avoidance, fear of change, fear of loss |
| **Nine of Cups** | Satisfaction, emotional stability, luxury | Lack of inner joy, smugness, dissatisfaction |
| **Ten of Cups** | Inner happiness, fulfillment, dreams coming true | Shattered dreams, broken family, domestic disharmony |
| **Page of Cups** | Happy surprise, dreamer, sensitivity | Emotional immaturity, insecurity, disappointment |
| **Knight of Cups** | Following the heart, idealist, romantic | Moodiness, disappointment, unrealistic |
| **Queen of Cups** | Compassion, calm, comfort | Martyrdom, insecurity, dependence |
| **King of Cups** | Compassion, control, balance | Coldness, moodiness, bad advice |

---

## 6. Suit of Swords (Air)

**Theme**: Intelligence, logic, truth, ambition, conflict, communication, mental challenges

| Card | Upright | Reversed |
|------|---------|----------|
| **Ace of Swords** | Breakthrough, clarity, sharp mind | Confusion, brutality, chaos |
| **Two of Swords** | Difficult choices, indecision, stalemate | Lesser of two evils, no right choice, confusion |
| **Three of Swords** | Heartbreak, suffering, grief | Recovery, forgiveness, moving on |
| **Four of Swords** | Rest, restoration, contemplation | Restlessness, burnout, stress |
| **Five of Swords** | Unbridled ambition, win at all costs, sneakiness | Lingering resentment, desire to reconcile, forgiveness |
| **Six of Swords** | Transition, leaving behind, moving on | Emotional baggage, unresolved issues, resisting transition |
| **Seven of Swords** | Deception, trickery, tactics and strategy | Coming clean, rethinking approach, deception |
| **Eight of Swords** | Imprisonment, entrapment, self-victimization | Self acceptance, new perspective, freedom |
| **Nine of Swords** | Anxiety, hopelessness, trauma | Hope, reaching out, despair |
| **Ten of Swords** | Failure, collapse, defeat | Can't get worse, only upwards, inevitable end |
| **Page of Swords** | Curiosity, restlessness, mental energy | Deception, manipulation, all talk |
| **Knight of Swords** | Ambition, action, drive, fast thinking | No direction, disregard for consequences, restlessness |
| **Queen of Swords** | Complexity, perceptiveness, clear mindedness | Cold-hearted, cruel, bitterness |
| **King of Swords** | Head over heart, discipline, truth | Manipulative, tyrannical, abusive |

---

## 7. Suit of Pentacles (Earth)

**Theme**: Material possessions, finances, career, health, nature, security, stability

| Card | Upright | Reversed |
|------|---------|----------|
| **Ace of Pentacles** | New opportunity, abundance, prosperity, security | Financial loss, missed opportunity, scarcity mindset |
| **Two of Pentacles** | Adaptability, juggling priorities, time management | Overwhelm, inability to balance, disorganization |
| **Three of Pentacles** | Collaboration, teamwork, learning, craftsmanship | Lack of teamwork, disregard for skills, poor quality |
| **Four of Pentacles** | Conservation, security, frugality, control | Greed, materialism, self-protection, hoarding |
| **Five of Pentacles** | Financial loss, poverty, isolation, hardship | Recovery from loss, spiritual poverty, hope returning |
| **Six of Pentacles** | Generosity, charity, sharing wealth, balance | Debt, selfishness, one-sided charity, strings attached |
| **Seven of Pentacles** | Long-term vision, perseverance, investment, patience | Impatience, poor returns, lack of reward, wasted effort |
| **Eight of Pentacles** | Apprenticeship, skill development, diligence, craftsmanship | Perfectionism, lack of ambition, misdirected effort |
| **Nine of Pentacles** | Luxury, self-sufficiency, financial independence | Over-investment in work, superficial, living beyond means |
| **Ten of Pentacles** | Legacy, inheritance, family wealth, long-term success | Financial failure, loss of legacy, family disputes |
| **Page of Pentacles** | Ambition, desire for knowledge, new financial opportunity | Lack of progress, procrastination, unrealistic goals |
| **Knight of Pentacles** | Hard work, productivity, routine, conservatism | Boredom, laziness, feeling stuck, perfectionism |
| **Queen of Pentacles** | Nurturing, practical, providing, security-oriented | Self-centeredness, jealousy, smothering, work-home imbalance |
| **King of Pentacles** | Wealth, business, leadership, discipline, abundance | Financially inept, obsessed with wealth, stubborn |

---

## 8. Numerological Patterns Across Suits

Each number carries consistent thematic weight across all four suits:

| Number | Theme | Wands (Fire) | Cups (Water) | Swords (Air) | Pentacles (Earth) |
|--------|-------|-------------|-------------|--------------|-------------------|
| **Ace** | Beginnings | Creative spark | Emotional opening | Mental clarity | Material opportunity |
| **2** | Duality/Choice | Planning | Partnership | Indecision | Juggling |
| **3** | Growth/Expansion | Foresight | Celebration | Heartbreak | Collaboration |
| **4** | Stability/Foundation | Community | Contemplation | Rest | Security |
| **5** | Conflict/Challenge | Competition | Loss | Ambition/defeat | Hardship |
| **6** | Harmony/Exchange | Victory | Nostalgia | Transition | Generosity |
| **7** | Reflection/Assessment | Perseverance | Illusion | Deception | Patience |
| **8** | Mastery/Movement | Speed | Walking away | Imprisonment | Craftsmanship |
| **9** | Culmination/Near-completion | Resilience | Satisfaction | Anxiety | Independence |
| **10** | Completion/Excess | Burden | Fulfillment | Defeat/ending | Legacy |

---

## 9. Elemental Interactions

Understanding elemental relationships is critical for game mechanics based on tarot:

| Interaction | Relationship | Game Design Implication |
|-------------|-------------|----------------------|
| Fire + Air (Wands + Swords) | **Complementary** - Fire feeds on air | Offensive synergy: damage + speed |
| Water + Earth (Cups + Pentacles) | **Complementary** - Water nourishes earth | Defensive synergy: healing + protection |
| Fire + Water (Wands + Cups) | **Opposing** - Fire evaporates water | Tension: passion vs. emotion |
| Air + Earth (Swords + Pentacles) | **Opposing** - Air erodes earth | Tension: thought vs. material |
| Fire + Earth (Wands + Pentacles) | **Neutral** - Fire tempers earth | Utility: creation + foundation |
| Water + Air (Cups + Swords) | **Neutral** - Water becomes mist | Utility: intuition + logic |

---

## 10. Tarot Symbolism Quick Reference

### Colors
| Color | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| **Red** | Passion, action, desire, blood, vitality |
| **Blue** | Intuition, spirituality, wisdom, water, calm |
| **Yellow/Gold** | Intellect, consciousness, solar energy, clarity |
| **Green** | Growth, fertility, nature, prosperity, healing |
| **White** | Purity, innocence, spirit, truth, potential |
| **Black** | Mystery, the unknown, death, transformation, void |
| **Purple** | Royalty, spirituality, higher knowledge, magic |

### Common Symbols
| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **Infinity (Lemniscate)** | Eternal cycles, limitless potential (Magician, Strength) |
| **Mountains** | Challenges, higher knowledge, spiritual achievement |
| **Water** | Emotions, the unconscious, purification, flow |
| **Pillars (B & J)** | Duality, threshold between worlds, balance of opposites |
| **Crown** | Authority, achievement, higher consciousness |
| **Angel** | Divine guidance, higher self, spiritual messenger |
| **Snake** | Transformation, knowledge, temptation, healing |
| **Rose** | Purity (white), passion (red), secrecy, beauty |
| **Laurel Wreath** | Victory, achievement, completion, honor |
| **Scales** | Justice, balance, truth, karma, equilibrium |

---

## 11. Relevance to Arcana Project

For a tarot-themed game, this reference enables:

- **Card effect design**: Each card's upright/reversed meaning maps to a positive/negative game effect
- **Elemental system**: Four suits = four damage/defense types with complementary/opposing interactions
- **Progression narrative**: The Fool's Journey provides a natural campaign arc (0 -> 21)
- **Numerological balance**: Cards of the same number across suits share thematic weight, enabling cross-suit synergies
- **Court card hierarchy**: Page -> Knight -> Queen -> King maps to unit tier progression
- **Reversed mechanics**: Every card having an upright AND reversed meaning creates a natural dual-use system
- **Symbolic theming**: Rich iconography provides visual and narrative design direction for each card
